description_long: This object is a simple, rectangular gravestone or headstone, typically found in cemeteries or graveyards. It consists of a thick, upright slab with a slightly arched top, mounted on a broad, flat rectangular base. The entire structure appears to be made from a single piece of gray stone, likely granite or concrete, with a uniform, matte finish. There are no visible inscriptions, engravings, or decorative elements on the surface, giving it a plain and unadorned appearance. The stone's surface texture is slightly rough, consistent with unfinished or lightly polished stone. Its size and proportions suggest it is intended as a traditional grave marker for a single burial plot.
description: A plain, gray stone headstone with a slightly arched top and a broad rectangular base, unadorned and typical of traditional grave markers.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the headstone with its arched top and wide rectangular base.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the upright slab and the depth of the base.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the plain back of the headstone and base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thickness and depth of the stone structure.
